passages.title: A new stage in the study and teaching of history
passages.source_html:
A new stage in the study and teaching of history


For hundreds of years, historians have relied on written or printed documents to provide the bulk of their source materials, and they have largely communicated with students and the wider public by writing books and journal articles. Today, however, the printed word is being superseded by a diversity of forms of communication, above all moving images on video or film.


A


The development of this new form of communication is leading to a growing gap between the practice of professional historians based in academia, and the practice of those aiming to popularise the study of history among the general public, and to encourage people to create their own records for the future. On the one hand, there are mainstream academics who continue to use only the written word as they examine more and more fields with an ever-increasing number of sophisticated methodologies. On the other hand, film and video, especially as broadcast on television, are probably the major influence on the public's consciousness of history, as they see film of events of fifty or a hundred years ago, events they had previously only read about.


B


In a related development, a great many people now document local and family events in the form of videos; many schools, too, produce video yearbooks. All these visual records may well prove to be invaluable sources of information for future historians. The glaring contradiction is that the two approaches – the academic and what we might term the popular – have intersected very little: with a few notable exceptions, professional historians have tended to avoid involvement in television programmes about history, and have even less impact on what is being captured and preserved on video. And the potential of moving images has wielded negligible influence on the academic study of history.


C


This gulf can be seen as resulting from the willingness or otherwise of individual historians to accept the validity of new forms of communication in the study of history. This is not the first time that the question has arisen. The study of history, as conceived of today, began with the transition from oral to literate culture, leading to the earliest written records and the earliest historical studies. The next great shift came with the advent of printing, which transformed everything. Today, as the printed word loses its dominance, historians are faced with a variety of forms of communication, ranging from simple audiotape to the promising complexities of videodiscs linked with computers. As yet, however, the use of moving images to record current events for the benefit of future historians does not even have a commonly agreed name.


D


This does not mean that mainstream historians have totally rejected the use of moving images as sources: the majority seem intrigued by the idea, and valuable research has been carried out into the history and analysis of films with a broad circulation, using them as a source of information on the social and intellectual history of the twentieth century. Journals such as American History Review have played a significant role in this field.


E


Yet the number of historians using moving images in their research or teaching is very small. The barrier seems to be that the profession is structured around the medium of the written word, and is somewhat insulated in its academic setting. The use of moving images presents a substantial challenge to this setting and its assumptions. As a result, historians have rejected the training, the institutions, the motivations and the professional structures that would be needed in order to use moving images effectively. Above all, they have rejected the necessity to learn complicated new skills.


F


So why should historians make this change? Clearly, films or videos of events and people can be used as solid evidence of the past, linked to the words of the narrator (whether a television presenter/historian or a university teacher giving a lecture) but carrying information in their own right. Film has reintroduced the oral form as a mode of research and communication for documenting historical events. Now, with moving images, people are reminded that oral communication is not limited to words: it also includes body language, expression and tone, and is embedded in a context. Little of this is evident in a written transcript. A further effect of video and film is that the narrator gives up some control and has less need to give explanations, while the viewer becomes involved in the process of interpreting and understanding history.


G


Film or videotape can also aid historians by simplifying the work of the interviewer. Instead of trying to carry on an interview while simultaneously making notes about setting and other unspoken data, this new kind of historian can concentrate on the interview itself, and study the film later. The many benefits of using moving images as historical evidence easily outweigh worries about cost, technical skills, or the effect of a camera on a person telling his or her story. Moving images enhance the quality of historical research, and suggest new directions for historians to explore.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27 - 35


Look at the seven paragraphs (A – G). Which paragraph contains the following information?

Choose the correct letter (A – G). You may choose any letter more than once.

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
27

27 an overview of the range of methods that have been used over time to document history
✅ 正确答案：C

定位关键词：

methods；document history


原文：

C段，“ The study of history, as conceived of today, began with the transition from oral to literate culture, leading to the earliest written records and the earliest historical studies. The next great shift came with the advent of printing, which transformed everything. Today, as the printed word loses its dominance, historians are faced with a variety of forms of communication, ranging from simple audiotape to the promising complexities of videodiscs linked with computers.”


解析：

题干信息是随着时间的推移用于记录历史的各种方法的概述，原文C段提到历史研究从口头过渡到书面，讲到印刷术的出现，最后提到录音带到视频光盘，所以该题与本段落信息相对应。
28

28 the main reason why many historians are unwilling to use films in their work
✅ 正确答案：E

定位关键词：

unwilling to use film


原文：

E段，“Above all, they have rejected the necessity to learn complicated new skills.”


解析：

题干信息为许多历史学家不愿意在他们的研究中使用电影的主要原因，E段最后一句话指出他们拒绝了学习复杂新技能的必要性，以及整个段落都在讲述动态图像对历史研究会形成障碍。
29

29 a reference to some differences between oral and written communication
✅ 正确答案：F

定位关键词：

differences；oral and written communication


原文：

F段，“Now, with moving images, people are reminded that oral communication is not limited to words: it also includes body language, expression and tone, and is embedded in a context. Little of this is evident in a written transcript.”


解析：

题干信息为口头和书面交流的不同点，原文F段提到口头交流并不局限于文字，还包括肢体语言、表情和语气等特点在书面记录中都不明显，即表明两者间存在差别。
30

30 how most citizens today gain an understanding of history
✅ 正确答案：A

定位关键词：

citizens；today；understanding of history


原文：

A段，“On the other hand, film and video, especially as broadcast on television, are probably the major influence on the public's consciousness of history”


解析：

原文提到电影、录像、电视可能是对公众历史意识的主要影响，表明现在人们大多都是从这些媒介上获取对历史的了解，所以此题信息存在于A段。
31

31 how current student events are sometimes captured for future audiences
✅ 正确答案：B

定位关键词：

current student event；future audiences


原文：

B段，“many schools, too, produce video yearbooks. All these visual records may well prove to be invaluable sources of information for future historians.”


解析：

题干信息为如何为未来的观众捕捉当前的学生事件，原文B段提到许多学校会制作视频年鉴，这些会成为未来历史学家宝贵的信息来源，此处信息与题干对应。
32

32 mention of the fact that the advantages of film are greater than the disadvantages
✅ 正确答案：G

定位关键词：

advantages；greater than the disadvantages


原文：

G段，“The many benefits of using moving images as historical evidence easily outweigh worries about cost, technical skills, or the effect of a camera on a person telling his or her story.”


解析：

题干信息为电影的优点大于缺点，原文G段提到使用动态影像作为历史证据的诸多好处远远超过了对成本、技术技能或摄影机对一个人讲述他或她的故事的影响的担忧，表明电影的好处比缺点更多，题干信息与此段对应。
33

33 the claim that there is no official title for film-based historical work
✅ 正确答案：C

定位关键词：

official title；film-based historical work

原文：

C段，“As yet, however, the use of moving images to record current events for the benefit of future historians does not even have a commonly agreed name.”


解析：

题干说电影改编的历史作品没有官方名称，原文C段最后一句提到然而，到目前为止，为了未来的历史学家的利益，使用动态图像来记录当前的事件，甚至没有一个普遍同意的名称，即没有一个官方名称。所以此题干信息在C段。


同义替换：

official title=commonly agreed name
34

34  reference to the active role the audience plays when watching films
✅ 正确答案：F

定位关键词：

requirements；obstacle


原文：

F段，“A further effect of video and film is that the narrator gives up some control and has less need to give explanations, while the viewer becomes involved in the process of interpreting and understanding history.”


解析：

题干信息为观众在观看电影时所扮演的积极角色，原文F段提到观众在看视频或电影的时候会参与到解释和理解历史的过程中，即观众在这个过程发挥了重要作用，此题信息与F段对应。
35

35 a list of requirements that historians see as obstacles to their use of film to record history
✅ 正确答案：E

定位关键词：

requirements；obstacle


原文：

E段，“The barrier seems to be that the profession is structured around the medium of the written word, and is somewhat insulated in its academic setting. The use of moving images presents a substantial challenge to this setting and its assumptions. As a result, historians have rejected the training, the institutions, the motivations and the professional structures that would be needed in order to use moving images effectively. ”


解析：

题干说历史学家认为这些要求是阻碍他们用电影记录历史的障碍，原文E段提到历史学专业是围绕书面文字的媒介构建的，并且在学术环境中有些绝缘，而动态图像的使用对此提出了重大挑战，所以历史学家拒绝了有效使用动态图像所需的培训、制度、动机和专业结构这一系列要求，所以此题信息与E段对应。


同义替换：

berrier=obstacle

Questions 36 - 40


Choose YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer, choose NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer, or choose NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

36

The needs of students in school have led to improvements in the teaching of history.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

Q36, NG

定位关键词：

needs of students；improvements；teaching in the history


原文：

The development of this new form of communication is leading to a growing gap between the practice of professional historians based in academia, and the practice of those aiming to popularise the study of history among the general public, and to encourage people to create their own records for the future.


解析：

题干说学生在学校的需求导致了历史教学的改进。但原文只提到视频和电影正在取代书本和期刊等印刷材料，成为历史学家传递历史相关的信息的最主要材料。这种新型沟通方式的发展正在导致学术界的专业历史学家与那些旨在让普通大众更广泛地学习历史，并鼓励人们为未来创作自己记录的群体之间的差距逐渐加大。原文并未提到学生在学校的需求，也没有提到其改进了历史教学方式。


37

Academic and popular historians have different attitudes towards the value of innovations in communication.

正确答案： A. YES

Q37, Y

定位关键词：

academic and popular historians；different attitudes；innovations in communication


原文：

A段，“The development of this new form of communication is leading to a growing gap between the practice of professional historians based in academia, and the practice of those aiming to popularise the study of history among the general public, and to encourage people to create their own records for the future.”


解析：

题目说学术史家和大众史家对交流创新的价值持有不同态度，原文A段提到了新型交流方式的发展导致学术界的专业历史学家的实践与旨在向公众普及历史研究、鼓励人们为未来创造自己的历史记录的实践之间的差距越来越大，以及后文写了部分人只使用书面文字，其他人会使用电影等，都表明两类史学家对传播方式创新价值的态度是不同的。



38

It is common for historians to play a major role in creating historical documentaries for television.

正确答案： B. NO

Q38, N

定位关键词：

historians；historical documentaries for television


原文：

B段，“with a few notable exceptions, professional historians have tended to avoid involvement in television programmes about history”


解析：

题干说历史学家在为电视制作历史纪录片方面发挥重要作用是很常见的，原文B段提到专业历史学家倾向于避免参与有关历史的电视节目，所以此题答案为NO。


同义替换：

historical documentaries for television=television programmes

39

Articles in American History Review have explored aspects of modern history through popular films.

正确答案： A. YES

Q39, Y

定位关键词：American History Review


原文：

D段，“the majority seem intrigued by the idea, and valuableresearch has been carried out into the history and analysis of films with a broad circulation, using them as a source of information on the social and intellectual history of the twentieth century. Journals such as American History Review have played a significant role in this field.”


解析：

根据定位词定位到D段，原文提到人们对广泛流传的电影的历史和分析进行了有价值的研究，把它们作为20世纪社会史和思想史的信息来源，American History Review等期刊在这一领域发挥了重要作用。即表明American History Review通过电影探索了现代历史，题干与原文相符，答案为YES。


同义替换：

intellectual history of the twentieth century=modern history

40

Developments in technology are influencing a range of academic subjects.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

Q40, NG

定位关键词：academic subjects


原文：G段，“Moving images enhance the quality of historical research,and suggest new directions for historians to explore.”


解析：

题干说动态影像提高了历史研究的质量，为历史学家提出了新的探索方向，而原文只提到对历史研究产生很大影响，并未提到对其他一系列学科也产生影响，所以此题答案为NG。
